ops.title: ccxbench fixtures targeted by ccx-arm tasks must exceed LARGE_READ_BYTES (20KB) or integrity flags the runs mislabeled
ops.body:
A ccxbench task family only measures ccx if the ccx arm actually engages ccx. The integrity checker (bench/ccxbench/integrity.py) marks a ccx-arm run `ok: False` ("mislabeled") when no ccx call was made AND no guard fired — and the read guard pack only blocks unbounded Reads on files over `LARGE_READ_BYTES` = 20,000 bytes (plugin/hooks/common.py). The first stale_anchor smoke run (2026-07-07) failed its gate 2/6 because status.go was ~700 bytes: haiku just Read the file natively in both arms.

**Why:** small fixture → guard silent → model sidesteps ccx → integrity discards the run from the paired ledger; the family measures nothing.

**How to apply:** any fixture file a ccx-arm task targets must exceed 20KB (build it deterministically, e.g. `_status_go()`/`_status_filler` in bench/ccxbench/fixtures.py — const-block filler stays out of outline captures so prompts stay compact), keep every trimmed line unique for bare-anchor safety, and place targets deep so windowed Reads don't reveal them. After the fix, all 3 ccx-arm runs used real ccx calls and the gate passed. Related: [[bench-spawnllm-isolation]].
